So when I said earlier that Houdini undoubtedly rigged his dramatic demonstrations, obviously, we assume Bob Allen rigged his dramatic demonstrations. The fire walks rigged. The show of hands here isn't rigged, but we know the outcome. I know the list. I know how many are here from a foreign country before I do it. I know the longevity before I do it. I wouldn't do it if I didn't know what the outcome was going to be. So fundamentally they're all rigged. So when I say that, you have to ask yourself whether you have a squeamish feeling at all about the idea of rigging your dramatic demonstrations. So now let's talk about pure illusion. So what do most people think of Thomas Edison for? The light bulb? What else? 9999 attempts in order to solve persistence. Yeah, fabulous inventor, right? I mean, that's what most people have him connected to and what he went to great travail to have people connect him to. And the illusion was largely, not that he didn't invent things, but the illusion was largely perpetuated by all of the things we're talking about, including dramatic demonstrations. So what Edison was actually best at was what? Being Edison. And for what outcome? In money. That's what he was best at. He raised money like nobody's business. And in somewhere between 8 out of 10 and 9 out of 10 of the cases when he raised money, what happened to all the money and all the people who put it up? All the money disappeared. That's what happened in 8 or 9 out of the 10 cases. He was spectacularly unsuccessful overall and furthermore, a lot of people don't know, is the stuff that actually was most viable didn't often lend itself to really great dramatic demonstration. So Edison did dramatic demonstrations of stuff he was working on that he had no intention of developing that were not practical and he knew were never going to happen. And then after he got the dough, he moved it from the thing he had shown off that could never happen over to the practical thing and worked on what he wanted to work on. So the giant press conferences and the giant gatherings of what we today would call venture capitalists in order to demonstrate to them this whiz bang thing that he was working on, he was an illusion and it was an illusion. The ethics escape valve I guess was the movement of the money. Now the question is how do you feel about that? Are you squeamish at all about that? I don't, it's a rhetorical question, it's for you to think about. But if you delve into the Edison's of our history, the contemporary figures of today, the high earners, there is illusion in front of and wrapped around everything that they do. They necessarily negate value delivered out the back end. But there is illusion making it work. What business do most people think Trump is still in? Yeah of course they think he is in the real estate business. And he perpetuates the idea he is in the real estate business. You guys all know he is not in the real estate business. I mean conceptually we know he is in the Trump business but beyond that he is not in the real estate business. He is in the licensing business just like Gene Simmons is in the licensing business.
